pullRequest.title: Update cooperative_pong to v6
pullRequest.body:
# Description

Changes to env behavior:
* bounce_randomness now correctly preserves speed
  Previously, bounces incorrectly added speed. Now the the bounce
  only change the angle.
* Fixed incorrect termination
  The ball was not checked for exiting both vertical and horizontal
  dimensions at the same time. This led to case where the ball was out of
  bounds but the game continued for an extra step.

Other changes:
* Type hinting was added
* Additional tests of the ball motion were added
* Documentation was updated/fixed
* The manual policy now supports both paddles
* A warning is emmited if the ball speed is too high
  (if too fast, it can pass through the paddle)
* Removed redundant action bounds check (was in both
  the env and a wrapper)
* Removed unused code
* Made CakePaddle a subclass of Paddle
* Paddles now know which side they are on
* Replaced custom code with test numpy function
* Prefixed some properties with '_' to mark as internal 
* Updated rendering method for Ball.
* Ball, Paddle, and CakePaddle now do their own reset
  (parameters to functions changed)

Closes #1286, Closes #1287, Closes #1288


## Type of change

- Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- This change requires a documentation update

# Checklist:

- [x] I have run the [`pre-commit` checks](https://pre-commit.com/) with `pre-commit run --all-files` (see `CONTRIBUTING.md` instructions to set it up)
- [x] I have run `pytest -v` and no errors are present.
- [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [x] I solved any possible warnings that `pytest -v` has generated that are related to my code to the best of my knowledge.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [x] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
